methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: planqc

data.narrative:
Quantum Motion is a Series A quantum computing company founded by John Morton and Simon Benjamin, with $60M+ raised to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparative universe of 11 peers across quantum computing: three public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave) and eight private firms including Quantinuum and Infleqtion.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using the $60M total raised as a valuation proxy for the Series A stage, a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier to anticipated Series B-stage value yields $120M. The Peer-Median method could not be applied: while seven peers exist in the quantum computing sector, the narrow peer set (filtered by stage, timing, and valuation tier) yielded insufficient datapoints—zero same-stage, one adjacent-stage, zero similar-tier—falling short of the three-minimum threshold required for reliable median benchmarking.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $102M to $138M, with a median point of $120M. This range reflects 15% downside and upside sensitivity around the central Stage-Progression estimate. Key uncertainties include the lack of direct same-stage comparables to validate the 2.0x multiplier in quantum computing specifically, and the nascent, heterogeneous nature of the sector, which limits predictive power from public trading multiples. The analysis would strengthen materially with additional Series A or late-stage private quantum computing transactions.
